links.targetTitle: Live collaboration uses per-user op feeds plus GSOC pings to trigger debounced pulls.

global.title: Reactor operation-based model handles concurrent edits natively; no CRDT is needed.
global.content:
## Decision

Connect's live collaboration does **not** use a CRDT. The reactor's existing operation-based document model already resolves concurrent edits: each user produces an independent stream of ops, and `reactor.load()` applies remote ops with proper ordering via skip/noop/merge semantics.

## Why this works

- Ops are signed and attributed to their author, so provenance is preserved across peers.
- Each peer's feed is append-only and per-user, so there is no shared write cursor to contest.
- Reordering and merge semantics live in the document model, not in the transport — SwarmChannel only has to pull new batches and hand them to the reactor.

## UX consequence

Because Powerhouse editors are too varied (tables, visual canvases, dashboards, button grids) to support meaningful per-editor cursors, the **only live surface is the document toolbar's History view**: peers' ops appear there in real time, attributed to the correct signer. No per-editor presence, no collaborative cursors.
global.description: Each collaborator produces an independent op stream; reactor.load() applies remote ops with proper ordering via skip/noop/merge semantics, so no CRDT layer is required.
